hero.title: Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church
hero.subtitle: A faith rooted in apostolic tradition, history, and spiritual life passed through generations.

ethiopia.title: Orthodoxy in Ethiopia
paragraphs: Christianity reached Ethiopia in the early centuries through apostolic tradition. The Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church became a pillar of faith, culture, and national identity.
For centuries, the Church preserved scripture, tradition, and spiritual life, shaping Ethiopian history and civilization.

community.title: Global Orthodox Community
community.subtitle: Discover the nations where Orthodox Christianity has the strongest presence today.

ethiopia.name: Ethiopia
ethiopia.description: Ethiopia is one of the oldest Christian nations in the world, with a continuous Orthodox tradition dating back to the 4th century.

russia.name: Russia
russia.description: Russia has the largest Orthodox population in Europe, deeply influencing its culture, history, and national identity.

greece.name: Greece
greece.description: Greece is historically central to Orthodox theology and tradition, with a strong national Orthodox identity.

romania.name: Romania
romania.description: Romania maintains one of the highest percentages of Orthodox Christians in Europe.

serbia.name: Serbia
serbia.description: The Serbian Orthodox Church plays a central role in Serbian national and cultural identity.

bulgaria.name: Bulgaria
bulgaria.description: Orthodoxy has shaped Bulgarian history and spiritual life for over a millennium.

egypt.name: Egypt
egypt.description: Egypt is home to the Coptic Orthodox Church, one of the oldest Christian communities in the world.

ukraine.name: Ukraine
ukraine.description: Ukraine has a strong Orthodox heritage rooted in the baptism of Kyivan Rus.

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia.description: Georgia is one of the earliest Christian kingdoms with a deeply rooted Orthodox identity.

mary-of-zion.name: Church of St. Mary of Zion
mary-of-zion.significance: One of the most sacred churches in Ethiopian Orthodoxy, traditionally believed to house the Ark of the Covenant.

lalibela-churches.name: Rock-Hewn Churches of Lalibela
lalibela-churches.significance: Eleven monolithic, subterranean churches carved from volcanic rock in the 12th–13th centuries by King Lalibela as a 'New Jerusalem.' Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1978.

holy-trinity-addis.name: Holy Trinity Cathedral
holy-trinity-addis.significance: A major cathedral serving as a primary religious center, known for its architecture, stained glass, and as the burial place of Emperor Haile Selassie.

debre-birhan-selassie.name: Debre Birhan Selassie Church
debre-birhan-selassie.significance: Famed for its beautiful 17th-century paintings and ceiling adorned with faces of angels, representing a masterpiece of Ethiopian Orthodox art.

bole-medhane-alem.name: Bole Medhane Alem Cathedral
bole-medhane-alem.significance: The largest Orthodox cathedral in Ethiopia and the second largest in Africa, serving as a center of worship and spiritual life.

abune-yemata-guh.name: Abune Yemata Guh
abune-yemata-guh.significance: A stunning 6th-century rock church carved into a sheer cliff 2,580 meters above sea level, remaining a testament to ancient Ethiopian Orthodox engineering.

debre-libanos.name: Debre Libanos
debre-libanos.location: 100 km north of Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
debre-libanos.significance: One of Ethiopia's most historically significant monastic enclaves, established in 1284 by Saint Tekle Haymanot. For centuries, its chief abbot was the second most powerful official in the Ethiopian Church.

famous.title: Famous Figures of Orthodoxy
famous.subtitle: Saints and theologians who shaped Orthodox faith, worship, and tradition across centuries.

1.name: St. Tekle Haymanot
1.title: Founder of Debre Libanos
1.description: An influential Ethiopian saint who founded the monastery of Debre Libanos and is credited with revitalizing the church during the early Solomonic dynasty.

2.name: Abba Garima
2.title: One of the Nine Saints
2.description: One of the Nine Saints who founded the monastery in Adwa and is traditionally credited with creating the earliest illuminated manuscripts (Garima Gospels).

3.name: St. Yared
3.title: Father of Ethiopian Church Music
3.description: A legendary saint credited with creating the liturgical music, chant system (Zema), and musical notation of the Ethiopian Church, which is still used today.

4.name: St. Frumentius
4.title: First Bishop of Ethiopia
4.description: Credited with converting King Ezana of Axum to Christianity and becoming the first Bishop of Ethiopia, laying the foundation for the Ethiopian Orthodox Church.
